actionable_feedback: Add a 'proof of formulas' worksheet to safety_PLC_3.xlsx that exposes the formula text (or include an unprotected copy of the workbook). This can be a sheet that uses Excel's FORMULATEXT or an exported text/C SV of key formula cells so auditors can inspect cell formulas without specialized libraries.
Ensure the workbook is not protected/locked or provide an exported sheet (PDF or CSV) showing the critical formula cells and the Inputs sheet values so auditors can validate calculations directly.

Complete and include the memo's final Recommendation table (the preview was truncated). The table should explicitly map each scenario to a pass/fail status given resolved thresholds OR state the exact gating policy to apply once thresholds are provided.

Clarify grader/verifier metadata: remove contradictory claims and explicitly state whether semantic correctness was judged and what evidence (memo lines / actual_outputs.json rows / in-sheet formula snippets) supports any semantic scores.

Add a short appendix in expert_memo.md that cross-references the workbook sheet names and exact cell ranges used for key calculations (e.g., Scenarios!A2:G10; Inputs!A1:D10), and list the formula expressions used for post-remediation calculations and Feasible_By_Budget check.

Re-run verification after adding the proof-of-formulas and memo appendix, and include an exported actual_outputs.json (unchanged values are fine) so the verifier can re-check numeric consistency without needing openpyxl.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Good coverage of inputs, remediation rules, and calculation logic; preview truncated-complete the Recommendation table and include explicit cell-range references.

safety_PLC_3.xlsx: Generated via manual OpenXML assembly. Because openpyxl was unavailable in the grader runtime, provide a proof-of-formulas sheet or unprotected workbook view to make formula-level inspection immediate.
feedback_summary: Deliverables present and consistent with the provided memo and normalized actual_outputs. Deterministic artifact-contract checks passed. Two issues require remediation before final audit acceptance: 1) The grader/verifier could not parse the .xlsx binary in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so in-sheet formulas were not directly inspected. 2) Metadata contains inconsistent statements about whether semantic correctness was evaluated (some entries state semantic evaluation was not performed while others report semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score=1.0). The memo preview is helpful but truncated and the Recommendation table was not fully visible in the preview.
